items.title: Learning Engineering Virtual Institute (LEVI)
items.context: LEVI brought together practitioners and researchers to define learning engineering practice. Published foundational resources on LE process and evidence standards.

items.title: Ken Koedinger
items.context: Co-originator of learning engineering as a field. Creator of Cognitive Tutor. Pioneer of PSLC DataShop and LearnLab. Foundational theorist and practitioner.

items.title: Bror Saxberg
items.context: Early champion of learning engineering as a named field. Wrote influential 2016 Atlantic piece calling for learning engineers. Practitioner-bridge figure.

items.title: Jim Goodell
items.context: Co-founder of Learning Engineering book (IEEE Press). Co-editor of canonical LE textbook. Core IEEE ICICLE contributor.

items.title: IEEE ICICLE Annual Meeting
items.context: Annual community gathering for the IEEE learning engineering community. Standards, BoK, and credentialing discussions.

items.title: IEEE ICICLE
items.context: International Community for IEEE Learning Engineering. Primary professional home for LE. Developing BoK, standards, credentialing. Resources page is a primary seed source for this corpus.

items.title: Learning Engineering (IEEE Press textbook)
items.context: The canonical textbook of the field. Two-part structure. End notes are a primary seed source for citation expansion. IEEE Press, 2022.

items.title: IEEE ICICLE Resources Page
items.context: Curated resource list maintained by IEEE ICICLE community. Includes papers, tools, programs, and community resources. Lacks cohesion and learning journey structure — this corpus addresses that gap.

items.title: Saxberg 2016 Atlantic: 'Forgotten Role of Learning Engineer'
items.context: Canonical entry point essay calling for the learning engineering profession. Widely cited. Short. Often assigned reading.

items.title: ICICLE Body of Knowledge (BoK) — current draft
items.context: The field's emerging canonical definition document. Defines competency areas, vocabulary, and practice standards.

items.context: Source: CMU. Author(s): Herb Simon. Type: Report. Year: 1967.

items.context: Source: CMU, Open Simon. Author(s): CMU. Type: Video. Year: 2020.

items.title: CMU Learn Lab Learning Science and Engineering Seminar Series
items.context: Source: CMU Learn Lab Learning. Author(s): CMU Learn Lab Learning. Type: Talks. Year: 2022.

items.title: Crucible Cyber Platform Brochure: They have learning engineering as part of their process.
items.context: Source: Software Engineering Institute, CMU. Type: Brochure. Year: 2026.

items.title: ASU Learning Engineering Institute website
Leveraging Learning Engineering to Orchestrate and Enhance Learning
items.context: Source: part of ASU's Augmented Intelligence Workshop series.. Author(s): Danielle S. McNamara. Type: Talk - Video. Year: 2022.

items.title: ASU LE Research Network 2026 Convening
items.context: Source: ASU. Type: convenings. Year: 2026.

items.title: Learning Engineering for Online Education (Dede, Richards, Saxberg eds.)
items.context: Edited volume by Dede, Richards, and Saxberg — the primary book-length treatment of learning engineering applied to online education. Covers LE process, adaptive systems, evidence, and scale.

items.title: Examples of Roles & Expertise on an LE Team (ICICLE)
items.context: Google Doc describing the multidisciplinary roles typically needed on a learning engineering team: learning scientists, data analysts, instructional designers, engineers, subject matter experts.

items.title: Bror Saxberg — MIT Festival of Learning Keynote 2023 (YouTube)
items.context: Keynote address by Bror Saxberg at the 2023 MIT Festival of Learning. Covers the vision of learning engineering, the gap between learning science and practice, and the role of data-driven design.

items.title: QIP Learning Engineering Video Series (YouTube playlist)
items.context: YouTube playlist produced by Quality Information Partners featuring practitioners and researchers discussing learning engineering principles, case studies, and field development.

items.title: Invitation to Learning Engineering Webinar Series (ICICLE, YouTube)
items.context: YouTube series of ICICLE-hosted webinars introducing the learning engineering field to new practitioners, covering foundational concepts, tools, and community resources.

items.title: IEEE Learning Engineering Podcast
items.context: Official IEEE ICICLE podcast series featuring conversations with learning engineering researchers, practitioners, and field leaders. Available on PodServe and Spotify.

items.title: Silver Lining for Learning — Art & Science of Learning Engineering (podcast, 2023)
items.context: Episode of the Silver Lining for Learning podcast exploring learning engineering as both an art and a science — balancing rigorous measurement with creative instructional design.

items.title: Baker, Boser & Snow (2022) — Learning Engineering: A View on Where the Field Is At
items.context: Open-access article surveying the state of the learning engineering field as of 2022. Reviews definitions, practice norms, evidence standards, and key open problems.

items.title: Craig et al. (2023) — Learning Engineering Perspectives for Educational Systems
items.context: Multi-author article framing how learning engineering perspectives can transform educational systems at scale. Addresses policy, infrastructure, and community dimensions.

items.title: Lee (2023) — Learning Sciences & Learning Engineering: Natural or Artificial Distinction?
items.context: Scholarly article examining the relationship between learning sciences and learning engineering. Argues for integration rather than separation, tracing epistemological differences and convergences.

items.title: Kolodner (2023) — Learning Engineering: What It Is, Why I'm Involved
items.context: Personal essay and scholarly reflection by Janet Kolodner on the nature of learning engineering, its roots in learning sciences, and her reasons for engagement with the field.

items.title: 7 Things You Should Know About Learning Engineering (EDUCAUSE, 2018)
items.context: Accessible EDUCAUSE brief introducing learning engineering to higher-education technologists and administrators. Covers definition, typical activities, prospects, and challenges.

items.title: MIT LEAP Group — Learning Engineering and Practice
items.context: MIT research group applying learning engineering principles to university-level STEM education. Produces open courseware, edX materials, and research on evidence-based pedagogy at scale.

items.title: The Learning Agency — EdSurge LE Series
items.context: Collection of practitioner-oriented articles from The Learning Agency published in EdSurge. Covers LE field development, practitioner perspectives, and connections to education reform.

items.title: AECT Learning Engagement Activated Podcast — Jim Goodell Episode (2022)
items.context: Podcast episode featuring Jim Goodell discussing learning engineering principles, the ICICLE community, and the future of evidence-based learning design in professional development contexts.

items.title: Goodell (2019) — Are You Doing Learning Engineering or Instructional Design?
items.context: Practitioner article by Jim Goodell drawing the distinction between instructional design and learning engineering. Emphasizes data use, iteration, and empirical grounding as key differentiators.

items.title: Learning Engineering at a Glance (Schatz, Goodell & Kessler, Army Press, 2023)
items.context: Concise overview of learning engineering for military and defense training contexts, published by US Army Press. Bridges LE field concepts with high-consequence domain workforce applications.

items.title: Learning Engineering is a Team Sport (ICICLE presentation, 2024)
items.context: ICICLE presentation emphasizing the collaborative, multidisciplinary nature of learning engineering teams. Addresses how different domain experts contribute to LE projects and how to build effective teams.

items.title: Herb Simon (1967) — The Job of a College President
items.context: Archival essay by Herbert A. Simon — foundational thinker behind cognitive science and bounded rationality — on university leadership and the role of evidence in educational decision-making. Cited as a historical intellectual ancestor of learning engineering.

items.title: International Society for the Learning Sciences (ISLS)
items.context: Home of CSCL and ICLS conferences. Bridges learning science and design. Important for T01 foundation layer.

items.title: How People Learn I & II
items.context: Foundational synthesis of learning science for educators. Free online. Widely cited. Essential T01 anchor.

items.title: Learning Engineering Process Description & Diagram (ICICLE)
items.context: Official ICICLE description of the Learning Engineering process, including the iterative cycle diagram showing problem framing, design, measurement, and improvement steps.

items.title: Learning Engineering Case Guide 1.0 (ICICLE)
items.context: Google Slides deck walking through real LE case examples. Shows how the LE process is applied in practice: problem formulation, data collection, evidence-based iteration.

items.title: Five Whys Root-Cause Analysis Template (ICICLE)
items.context: Google Doc template for applying the Five Whys technique to identify root causes of learning performance gaps. Designed for the LE needs-analysis phase.

items.title: Fishbone / Cause-Effect Analysis Template (ICICLE)
items.context: Google Doc template for Ishikawa (fishbone) cause-and-effect diagrams in learning contexts. Used during LE problem framing and needs analysis to map contributing factors to learning gaps.

items.title: Performance Task Analysis Template (ICICLE)
items.context: Google Doc template for structured task decomposition in learning engineering. Guides practitioners through breaking complex performance into observable subtasks to inform instructional design.

items.title: Learning Engineering Implementation Checklist (ICICLE)
items.context: Google Doc checklist for practitioners implementing a learning engineering project. Covers project phases from needs analysis through evaluation and dissemination.

items.title: Learning Design Principles Toolkit (McEldoon, ICICLE)
items.context: Google Drive toolkit by Katherine McEldoon connecting cognitive science principles to practical learning design decisions. Bridges learning science theory and LE design practice.

items.title: Silver Lining for Learning — Human-Centered Methods in LE (podcast, 2023)
items.context: Episode of the Silver Lining for Learning podcast focused on human-centered design methods applied within the learning engineering process.

items.title: IDEO Design Thinking Process (ideou.com)
items.context: IDEO's publicly available overview of the human-centered design thinking process. Referenced in LE as a precursor and complement to the iterative LE process, particularly for empathy and problem framing stages.

items.title: Ryan Baker
items.context: Pioneer in educational data mining and learning analytics. Author of key EDM methods handbook. EDM journal editor.

items.title: John Behrens
items.context: Evidence-centered design; learning analytics at scale; psychometrics meets LE. Key bridge between assessment science and learning engineering.

items.title: Educational Data Mining (EDM)
items.context: Annual international conference and journal. Primary venue for learning analytics and EDM research.

items.title: Learning Analytics & Knowledge (LAK)
items.context: Premier analytics in education conference. Strong on data infrastructure and equity.

items.title: DataShop / LearnSphere
items.context: Largest open repository of learning interaction data. Enables corpus expansion and field-level analysis.

items.title: Society for Learning Analytics Research (SoLAR)
items.context: International nonprofit dedicated to research, practice, and policy of learning analytics. Home of LAK conference and companion journal.

items.title: Data Use Throughout the Learning Engineering Process (ICICLE graphic)
items.context: Infographic and supporting documentation mapping how different types of data — formative, summative, behavioral, survey — are collected and used at each stage of the LE process.

items.title: Build a Learning Data Dream Team (Torrance, Lin & Goodell, ATD, 2024)
items.context: Practitioner article on assembling multidisciplinary teams to leverage learning data effectively. Covers roles (data analyst, learning engineer, instructional designer) and collaborative workflows.

items.title: Van Campenhout et al. (2023) — Student-Centered Educational Data Science Through Learning Engineering
items.context: Research article integrating educational data science methods within a learning engineering framework. Demonstrates how student-centered analysis and iterative design reinforce each other in practice.

items.title: Developing an Ontology for Learning Engineering Learning Engineering Enlightenment: Think Like an Engineer
items.context: Source: New Frontiers website. Author(s): Ellen Wagner. Type: Blog Posts. Year: 2024.

items.title: Human-Computer Interaction Institute — CMU
items.context: Home of Ken Koedinger and Cognitive Tutor / LearnLab. One of the premier academic homes for LE research globally. PhD and MS programs.

items.title: Open Learning Initiative (OLI)
items.context: OLI is the flagship LE platform from CMU — courses built with LE principles, continuous improvement, embedded formative assessment, and learning analytics.

items.title: Learning @ Scale
items.context: Premier venue for LE research at scale. Emphasizes empirical evidence and adaptive/personalized learning. Annual.

items.title: ASSISTments
items.context: Free ITS platform widely used for math tutoring research. Large research base. RCTs conducted on platform.

items.title: OpenSimon Community (CMU Simon Initiative)
items.context: Open-access community and toolkit collection from CMU's Simon Initiative. Hosts LearnSphere DataShop, OLI course materials, and research infrastructure for learning engineering at scale.

sections.section: T07 AI & Foundation Models in Learning

items.title: Artificial Intelligence in Education (AIED)
items.context: Flagship AI in education conference. ITS sub-conference often co-located. Annual; alternating with ITS.

items.title: Playful Journey Lab / MITili (MIT)
items.context: MIT lab focused on game-based and playful learning engineering. MITili (MIT Integrated Learning Initiative) applies data-driven, iterative design to immersive and game-based educational experiences.

items.title: SchoolSims — LE-Based Teacher Preparation Simulator (2025)
items.context: Simulation platform for teacher preparation applying learning engineering principles, cognitive task analysis, and naturalistic decision-making. Uses scenario-based assessment and data-driven iteration.

items.title: State of XR & Immersive Learning Report 2021 (iLRN / ICICLE)
items.context: Annual state-of-the-field report on extended reality (XR/VR/AR) in learning contexts, co-produced with ICICLE. Covers research landscape, practitioner adoption, and evidence base for immersive learning.

items.title: LENS @ JHU — Learning Engineering for Next-Generation Systems
items.context: Concentration within JHU MEd in Learning Design & Technology. Targets practitioners in complex organizations: defense, healthcare, large-scale education. Grounded in human systems integration and learning engineering expertise. Capstone produces evidence dashboard, reproducible report, and governance/ethics plan. Unique JHU ecosystem: APL + Medicine + IEEE/ICICLE partnership.

items.title: Will Gray-Roncal
items.context: Principal PI of LENS @ JHU, CIRCUIT, MERIT programs. Connectomics and agentic AI researcher. Visiting Assistant Professor developing Learning Engineering Specialization at JHU.

items.title: Teaming Up to Improve Medical & Healthcare Education (Kurzweil & Marcellas, 2020)
items.context: Case study and practitioner article applying learning engineering team approaches to medical and healthcare professional education. Demonstrates LE in a high-stakes, complex domain.

items.title: Beyond Benchmarks: Responsible AI in Education Needs Learning Sciences
items.context: Source: Communication of ACM. Author(s): Jeremy Roschelle, Elizabeth A. McLaughlin, and Kenneth R. Koedinger. Type: article. Year: 2025.

items.title: Learning Engineering Evidence Decision Tracker (ICICLE)
items.context: Google Drive folder with templates for tracking design decisions alongside the evidence that motivated them throughout the LE process. Supports reproducible, evidence-based practice.

items.title: At the Empowering Learners for the Age of AI conference, there were two keynotes related to learning engineering and an ICICLE panel. The videos for all the sessions on the Global Research Alliance for AI in Learning Education's (GRAILE) YouTube channel. Danielle McNamara, University of Arizona, gave the opening keynote, Leveraging AI and Learning Engineering in Large Scale Learning Scienceshttps://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yn3AIuKJ1RY A panel of ICICLE folks spoke about Learning Engineering: A path to empowering learners in and for the Age of AI. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=H-MbNTt2NRc Kumar Garg, Schmidt Futures, shared his thoughts in his keynote on Learning Engineering in the Age of AI. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HCDis6ELscA
items.context: Source: Empowering Learnings in the Age of AI Conference. Year: 2022.

items.title: Generalizable Learning Engineering Adoption Maturity Model
items.context: PDF defining a maturity model for organizations adopting LE practices. Stages describe progression from ad hoc instructional design through fully evidence-based, data-driven learning improvement.

items.title: Learning Engineering Adjacent Academic Programs Directory (ICICLE)
items.context: Google Doc directory of academic programs adjacent to or incorporating learning engineering, including LE-specific degrees, learning sciences programs, educational technology programs, and HCI programs.

items.title: High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering (Baker & Boser, 2021)
items.context: Report by Ryan Baker and Beth Boser identifying the highest-leverage opportunities for learning engineering investment. Covers evidence gaps, scale barriers, and field infrastructure needs.
